Concrete walls, military checkpoints, surveillance algorithms: borders have a thousand faces, and none of them are truly alike. In this edition of Entre Nous we speak to the curator of the show, Delphine Heyndrickx. She tells us how her team came up with the idea and what the different sorts of borders can be.

description: Muzarabani ‌had agreed to join Islamabad United but switch to Indias Kolkata Knight Riders led to Pakistan cricket ban.

title: Основатель самого закредитованного застройщика мира признал вину в мошенничестве
description: Основатель самого закредитованного застройщика мира China Evergrande Хёй Каянь признал вину в махинациях с привлечением капитала, нецелевом расходовании средств и ряде других преступлений, пишет Reuters со ссылкой на сообщение Народного суда промежуточной инстанции города Шэньчжэнь в его официальном аккаунте в WeChat.

title: Who still reads Holocaust stories? A publisher sees shifting trends after October 7
description:
Demand for Holocaust memoirs is falling amid rising antisemitism and changing consumer patterns. A non-Jewish Dutch woman has made it her mission to keep survivors' voices on shelves

title: Swedish Armed Forces Release Photo Showing JAS 39 Gripens Escorting Russian Kilo-Class Submarine
description: The Russian submarine shadowed by the Swedish Gripens was transiting the Kattegat, a strategic waterway linking the Baltic approaches with the North Sea system. Although the spotlight is currently on Iran, the Russian military activity in the Baltic region continues to draw scrutiny. title: The president who threatened to end a civilization is supposed to guarantee Ukraine’s survival
description: Trump's Iran war has diverted diplomats and drained interceptor stockpiles — just as his envoys were supposed to deliver security guarantees to Kyiv.
